narrative: OMX30 opens in a structural bull market (+8.0% vs 200-MA) but technical momentum is diluted by weak ADX (9.3), signaling low conviction despite MACD above signal and neutral RSI (61.35). EUR/SEK weakness and USD/SEK strength provide tailwinds for export-heavy industrials and pharma; however, the 2.48% oil surge creates headwinds for input-intensive sectors and consumer discretionary. Geopolitical risk (US-Iran fire exchange) has lifted VIX 171bps and yields have compressed 45bps, triggering defensive positioning. Sector rotation toward AstraZeneca/Essity and mining (via gold stability) is likely, while banks face offsetting pressures: wider spreads from elevated rates offset by falling yields damaging loan pricing momentum.
morningBrief: OMX30 should open modestly positive (+0.3 to +0.5%) on the back of USD strength and technical above-200-MA positioning, but ADX weakness at 9.3 demands caution — don't chase rallies in a low-conviction regime. Exporters (Volvo B, Atlas Copco A, Ericsson B) are favored by USD/SEK 9.369 and should outperform, but watch for mean reversion if energy costs spill into margin expectations. Brent +2.48% is a red flag for industrials input costs; monitor Sandvik and SSAB A for cost-push signals. Banks (SEB A, Handelsbanken A) face mixed signals: rate spreads widen but falling yields compress DCF. Geopolitical risk (VIX +171bps) suggests accumulating AstraZeneca and Essity on weakness — defensive rotation is live. Key level to defend: 3123 (yesterday close); break below 3085 (lower Bollinger) signals momentum exhaustion. Watch US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET for late-session volatility.
drivers: Weak ADX (9.3) limits upside conviction despite bull regime
USD/SEK strength (+0.67%) supports major exporters (Volvo, Atlas Copco, Ericsson)
Oil spike (+2.48%) pressures industrials and consumer spending but supports energy allocations
Geopolitical escalation (US-Iran tensions) creates VIX uptick (+1.71%) — defensive tilt favored
US yields down 45bps supports tech valuations but mixed for bank NIM expansion

sectorImpacts.reason: USD/SEK +0.67% boosts export pricing power, but Brent +2.48% raises input cost pressure. Net: slight headwind for margin-sensitive sub-segments.
US 10Y yield down 45bps (negative for loan pricing) partially offset by elevated rate volatility (VIX +1.71%, supports option revenue). Net: flat to slightly negative.
Geopolitical risk escalation (US-Iran tensions) triggers defensive rotation; lower yields support biotech valuation multiples. Risk-off environment favors counter-cyclical exposure.
NASDAQ flat (+0.03%) removes negative sentiment drag. Falling yields (+4.455 to lower) support growth multiples, but weak ADX limits upside enthusiasm. Hold.
Gold +0.03% and copper -0.44% are mixed, but geopolitical premium in precious metals supports Boliden long-term. SSAB pressured by oil/energy costs.
Brent +2.48% increases fuel/transport costs, pressuring consumer discretionary spending. EUR/SEK weakness (+0.47%) raises H&M sourcing costs. Avoid on weakness.
USD/SEK +0.67% is a strong tailwind for export-heavy Volvo B; Volvo Cars B benefits from strong Nordic export demand and currency upside.
Yields down 45bps supports refinancing, but VIX spike and risk-off sentiment typically pressures property valuations. Monitor closely.

watchList.reason: USD/SEK +0.67% is a direct earnings tailwind; stock trades above 200-MA with export cycle support. Entry on any dip toward 242 SEK.
USD strength positive, but oil-driven input cost inflation (Brent +2.48%) may pressure margins. Wait for company guidance confirmation.
Geopolitical risk escalation and falling yields support defensive biotech rotation. Accumulate on any weakness toward 9800 SEK.
Gold +0.03% and geopolitical premium support long-term positioning, but copper -0.44% is a minor headwind. Monitor commodity mix.
Falling yields (10Y -45bps) compress loan pricing margins faster than deposit rate compression can offset. Avoid on strength.
EUR/SEK weakness (+0.47%) raises Asian sourcing costs; Brent +2.48% pressures transport. Risk/reward unfavorable until macro stabilizes.

econCalendarAlert: No Swedish macro releases. US Jobless Claims (initial & continuing) at 14:30 CET — potential volatility driver if claims spike on recession fears.
topRisk: Geopolitical escalation (US-Iran fire exchange, OECD 'dark scenario' warning on Gulf energy crisis) could trigger VIX spike beyond 18, forcing risk-off liquidation across all risk assets. Watch for any additional headlines; breakeven on USD/SEK support may collapse if crisis spreads.
technicalSummary: ADX 9.3 = WEAK conviction — reduce position size and tighten stops
MACD above signal (hist +0.883) = bullish structure, but RSI 61.35 = neutral (no overbought)
Bollinger %B 0.62 (upper half) = trending but not extended — room to run on breakout
52W 89% of range, -2.8% from all-time high = near extremes; pullback risk elevated
Stochastic K=63.9, D=60.6 = neutral; no mean reversion trigger yet
